narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—dreaming of law school and then hearing you'll likely face a tough road with the bar exam can feel like a gut punch, especially with your full-time commitment and clear passion for law. Our prediction model looked at your profile and saw a 40% chance of not passing the bar, painting a picture of someone who's capable but starting from a challenging spot academically. The biggest signal was your Year 3 decile of 2, which stood out as the strongest pull toward struggle, suggesting those later years in school didn't build the momentum needed for the bar's intensity. Right behind that was your LSAT score of 29, which, while solid for some paths, signaled to the model a hurdle in the kind of analytical firepower the exam demands. Your Year 1 decile of 1 echoed that early pattern, reinforcing a story of consistent underperformance relative to peers. Even with a middle family income and a 3.0 undergrad GPA—respectable markers of grit—they couldn't quite offset those academic flags in the model's view. Factors like being a white female pursuing full-time studies played smaller roles, but together they all wove a cautious tale of risk. This isn't about your worth or potential; it's the model spotting patterns from thousands of similar journeys where these pieces often led to bar setbacks. The good news? You're in control of rewriting this story—targeted prep could shift those deciles and LSAT edges in your favor for another shot. You've got the drive; now channel it where it counts most. Keep pushing—you're closer than this one prediction suggests.
